rationale.https://www.manning.com/books/python-con: Manning publisher page — canonical source for 'Python Concurrency with asyncio' (Fowler), the definitive book on this exact topic. Authoritative primary source.
rationale.https://www.oreilly.com/library/view/pyt: O'Reilly canonical listing — second-most authoritative book platform, full coverage of asyncio event loop model and concurrency landscape.
rationale.https://superfastpython.com/asyncio-even: Direct tutorial on asyncio event loop in separate thread — precisely on-topic for 'event loop concurrency'. Free content, high practical value.
rationale.https://superfastpython.com/asyncio-mult: Direct tutorial on running multiple concurrent event loops — core scalability concern in asyncio concurrency. On-topic.
rationale.https://books.google.com/books/about/Pyt: Google Books entry — broad accessibility, same authoritative book. Useful for users who want preview/overview.
rationale.https://pythonbooks.org/python-concurren: Book review and summary on pythonbooks.org — useful editorial perspective on the book's scope and content.
rationale.https://pybitesbooks.com/books/On9dEAAAQ: Pybites Books listing with community context — another platform covering the Fowler asyncio book. Lower uniqueness than publisher pages.
rationale.https://superfastpython.com/python-async: Curated list of asyncio books — meta-resource that points to the field's key references. Useful for discovery beyond the Fowler book.
rationale.https://www.amazon.com/Python-Concurrenc: Amazon product page — retail listing, reader reviews, table of contents. Lower informational value than publisher/O'Reilly pages.
rationale.https://www.simonandschuster.com/books/P: Simon & Schuster publisher retail page — lowest uniqueness of the set, but valid on-topic listing.

notes: Pool is extremely homogeneous — 7 of 10 URLs are variations of the same 'Python Concurrency with asyncio' (Fowler) book across retail/platform pages. Ranking prioritizes publisher/authoritative platforms first, then SuperFastPython tutorials (unique content), then retail duplicates. All 10 included since pool size equals top-10 limit.
